subject: PNF WF post instantiation configuration
commit_message:
PNF WF post instantiation configuration

Fix the PNF registration issue
Use the applicationEvent to notify about PNF ID

Issue-ID: SO-1506
Change-Id: I4c5ebfdd4ed319408ef99e1360c37124688fd7f1
Signed-off-by: eeginux <henry.xie@est.tech>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Hi All,

Please review the solution for the problem of PNF registration mentioned in the mail from Lukasz. 

I quickly push this one for review purpose, and I will rebase to latest commit as I have 'formatting' issue in the latest commit. And I will work on Junit tests as well.

Patch Set 3:

> Was was this change made?

Hi Lukasz,

instead of running the thread to do message correlation, I changed to use spring applicationEvent to notify about the incoming message. 

The main issue with runnable.run() is the thread is not released( =null), and all the following activities will be executed in both the main thread and the thread started by runnable.run().  

I haven't gotten the stable environment to test it e2e, and I am trying to set that up. 

there are few code change i need to make, I will raise a bug to track that.

Patch Set 3:

> > Was was this change made?
 > 
 > Hi Lukasz,
 > 
 > instead of running the thread to do message correlation, I changed
 > to use spring applicationEvent to notify about the incoming
 > message.
 > 
 > The main issue with runnable.run() is the thread is not released(
 > =null), and all the following activities will be executed in both
 > the main thread and the thread started by runnable.run().
 > 
 > I haven't gotten the stable environment to test it e2e, and I am
 > trying to set that up.
 > 
 > there are few code change i need to make, I will raise a bug to
 > track that.

Hi,

What do you mean by that it will be executed twice? This runnable is mererly a lambda expression that should be executed when certain message is received. It should not be executed in any other thread.

I analized your implementation futhere and I noticed a critical bug - you're saving execution in InformDmaapClient - THIS CLASS SHOULD BE STATELESS!!! If you're saving execution in it there is (very high) chance that wrong execution will be used! This change breaks critivcal functionality - no more than one pnf can wait for activation.

Patch Set 3:

Hi Lukasz,

As said I am not intended to break the PNF registration flow. I won't change the existing code unless i have issue. 

Could you please try to checkout https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/84105/ and change PnfEventReadyDmaapClient to runnable.run()? You can run/debug CreateVcpeResCustServiceSimplifiedTest and there should be exception raised about grpc too many header or similiar. Searching the output log for 'Message received:', and you will see that message twice from two different threads. This is the thread issue I mentioned. 

Regarding the more than one pnf activation scenario, my understanding is the current BPMN handling one PNF resource per PNF service, it doesn't handle the case of muliple PNF resources. Would it happen that more than one PNF activation is processed within one camunda process instance? 

About the InformDmaapClient state, I will see what can be done for that.

To properly fix the issue, I am trying to understand the logic of PnfEventReadyDmaapClient and InformDmaapClient. My understanding is for the incoming pnfCorrelationId, once PNF_READY message is received, correlate the WorkflowMessage so receive task(Wait for DMAAP pnf-ready notification) can awake and continue. Is that right? 

I will raise a bug to track the change.
